[high] D3: Strengthen anti-patterns with more non-obvious entries — current set (happy path only, vague steps) is well-known; add expert-level anti-patterns like testing implementation details instead of behavior, or over-mocking hiding integration bugs
[high] D8: Add a complete worked example showing a real test plan output — the skill teaches how to think but never shows a finished artifact
[low] D1: Trim activation-level content (acceptance criteria validation section) and replace with more expert heuristics like defect clustering analysis or risk-based test selection algorithms
[high] D4: Narrow the description trigger — 'Use when writing or reviewing tests' is too broad; specify Python/pytest context to avoid false-positive routing for non-Python projects

Add filename examples for each category - a quick table showing essential/relevant/branch/idea/experimental filename patterns would improve practical usability
Add brief TOC - at 169 lines with multiple sections, a 5-line navigation table would improve discoverability
Strengthen why for category philosophy - add 1-2 sentences explaining the underlying principle (auto-load budget, context window conservation)
Deduplicate credential paths between Read/Bash sections into a shared array to reduce 4-place edits when extending
Add documented test cases in header comments (echo pipe examples for block and allow scenarios, like block-dangerous-commands.sh does)
Catch .env.* variants (e.g. .env.production) in the Bash section regex and add .sample to the allowlist to match HOOKS.md documentation
Add explicit allowlist mechanism (env var or array) for project-specific safe exceptions instead of relying solely on pattern scoping
Resolve overlap with enforce-uv-run: document the interaction or consolidate guidance so users don't get conflicting block messages
Add configurable safety level (block vs warn mode) via env var or constant
Implement blocking for pip/pip3/pytest/ruff/mypy as documented in HOOKS.md, or update HOOKS.md to reflect actual scope -- the spec/implementation gap costs D1 and D6 points
Add a PATTERNS array (like enforce-make-commands.sh) instead of inline regex to make extension easier and support allowlisting safe exceptions (D3/D4)
Add allowlist mechanism for safe python invocations (e.g., python -c one-liners, python -m json.tool) to reduce false positives (D3)
Add test cases for edge cases: missing plan title (fallback slug), cp failure, and mkdir failure
Fix GNU sed portability: replace `s/^-\|-$//g` alternation with two separate sed expressions for macOS compatibility
Enrich HOOKS.md description to mention slug generation, env var configuration, and the timestamp naming convention
Extract branch-checking logic (git repo check, get branch, detached HEAD, protected check) into a shared function to eliminate duplication between EnterPlanMode and Bash handlers -- D4 +3
Add an allowlist or bypass mechanism for legitimate direct-to-main operations (e.g., hotfix commits) and add stderr logging for blocked actions -- D3 +3
Update HOOKS.md index to reflect both EnterPlanMode and Bash matchers and document the git commit blocking behavior -- D6 +2
Replace the fragile ls | xargs | basename | sed | grep pipeline (line 53) with a for-loop and parameter expansion for robustness and clarity (D1/D4)
Add stderr logging (e.g., >&2 echo) for diagnostic cases like jq not found, permission errors, or malformed learned.json to aid debugging (D3)
Update HOOKS.md description to cover all sections the hook actually outputs (lessons, guidance, main branch) not just essential memories and git branch (D6)

[high] Fix false positives from aggressive normalization -- stripping quotes globally means string arguments like echo with dangerous-looking text trigger the hook (demonstrated live: the hook blocked a jq command containing pattern text in a JSON string)
[high] Catch separated short flags (rm -r -f /) and long-form flags (rm --recursive --force /); current regex only matches adjacent -rf/-fr
[med] Extract dangerous patterns into a configurable array and deduplicate the rm -rf/-fr regex repeated 3 times across root/home/cwd checks
Handle non-existent files gracefully -- allow the read instead of blocking, since the file size cannot be determined (D1/D3)
Convert ALLOWED_FILES from comma-separated string to a bash array and unify with the *.config.json regex into a single allowlist mechanism (D4)
Add stderr logging when blocking (e.g., >&2 echo "suggest-read-json: blocked $FILE_PATH") for debugging false positives (D3)
Add single-redirect (>) and cp detection to Bash handler -- echo x > ~/.bashrc and cp payload ~/.bashrc both bypass the hook, losing D1 points
Unify BLOCKED_CONFIGS array and Bash CONFIGS regex into a single source of truth -- currently two independent lists that can drift, costing D4 points
Add documented test cases in header comments (block and allow examples) matching the pattern in block-dangerous-commands.sh -- currently no inline test commands
